data.narrative:
KoBold Metals is a Series C-stage Climate & Energy company that has raised $400M+ against a current post-money valuation of $2.0B+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA), spanning a valuation range of $120M to $6.0B. The peer cohort includes Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B Series C), Amogy ($700M Series C), Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B Series B), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B Series D).

Two triangulation methods are applied. The stage-progression method anchors to KoBold's last post-money valuation of $2.0B and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to next-stage progression, yielding $3.4B. The peer-median method identifies a median valuation of $500M across the nine-peer sample, reflecting the wide dispersion inherent in early-stage climate tech. This method yields a $500M implied value.

The blended triangulated range spans $425M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The $2.0B current valuation sits within this range but above the peer median, reflecting either a stage-progression premium or market expectations specific to KoBold's technology profile. Key uncertainties include the company's capital intensity trajectory, technology de-risking milestones, and near-term funding environment sensitivity typical of pre-revenue frontier tech.
